pattern: A monotonic stack keeps a character only while a smaller later letter still needs the spot and a spare copy remains, then a final trim drops any trailing repeat that only padded the length.
hints: Every distinct letter must survive at least once, but a letter with duplicates can end up appearing more than once in the answer too - so this is not the classic 'exactly one of each' problem.
Build a stack left to right: pop a bigger top letter only if it still has a copy left later (or another copy already in the stack) to satisfy its 'at least once' requirement.

Watch the tail: pushing a repeat of the current top with nothing smaller left to justify it just makes the string longer for no benefit, and a shorter prefix always beats a longer extension of it - trim trailing repeats that have another copy earlier in the stack.

edge_inputs.description: a duplicated letter should be fully trimmed once nothing follows it
trailing duplicate of an earlier letter, no distinct letters after it
keeping the extra duplicate changes which letter occupies the next slot
interleaved distinct letters repeating in a cycle
every character identical, collapses to one
strictly descending distinct letters: no letter repeats, nothing removable

stress.description: long string over a small alphabet with heavy repetition
stress.generator_python:
import string
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alphabet = string.ascii_lowercase[:rng.randint(2, 6)]
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
